title: Fix building against Python 3.8
body:
The behaviour of `python-config --libs` has changed in Python 3.8.

For example, compare the output of it with Python 3.7 and 3.8 on an ArchLinux system:

    $ python3.7-config --libs
    -lpython3.7m -lcrypt -lpthread -ldl  -lutil -lm
    $ python3.8-config --libs
    -lcrypt -lpthread -ldl  -lutil -lm -lm
    $

The lack of `-lpython` in the latter case causes the linker to fail when attempting to build Yosys against Python 3.8, with missing symbol errors. It also breaks the boost_python name detection.

Passing the new `--embed` flag to `python-config` adds `-lpython`, just like earlier versions of Python:

    $ python3.8-config --embed --libs
    -lpython3.8 -lcrypt -lpthread -ldl  -lutil -lm -lm
    $

This pull request adds code for automatically detecting support for the `--embed` flag. If it is supported, it is passed to all `python-config` invocations. This fixes building against Python 3.8, while maintaining compatibility with older versions.

There's a bit more information in the comments here: https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/yosys-git/
